title: bridge.result
description: What the sidecar returns to the desktop. The DESKTOP is the final receipt authority (design §3): it cryptographically verifies the receipt's Ed25519 signature over the canonical envelope against a pinned key — so this payload carries the SIGNED receipt material (`envelope_jcs_b64` + `signature_b64`) and NEVER a self-asserted `verified` boolean. Fail-closed: `result` is non-null ONLY when `ok` is true (a completed, evidenced run); a failure never carries a result. A missing/empty signature means the desktop Blocks — it does not mean the bridge asserts trust.

result.description: The AI turn's output. Non-null iff ok=true. The desktop still only RENDERS it if the receipt's signature verifies.

envelope_jcs_b64.description: base64url(JCS(receipt envelope)) — the exact canonical bytes the desktop verifies the signature over. `null` when the engine produced no signed receipt (e.g. Wave 3a has no isolated signer) → the desktop Blocks.

signature_b64.description: base64url(Ed25519 signature) over `envelope_jcs_b64`. `null` when unsigned → the desktop Blocks. The bridge NEVER asserts this is valid; the desktop verifies it.

containment_evidence_b64.description: Wave 3b (design §4.2, P1-3): base64url of the EXACT containment-evidence bytes the signer hashed into `containment_evidence_sha256`, so the desktop can persist the artifact for audit and re-check `sha256 == containment_evidence_sha256` without depending on the engine store. Capped at 64 KiB HERE (it does NOT ride the signer's 64 KiB IPC frame). `null`/absent when there is no signed receipt. The desktop ignores it until 3b-2 persistence lands.

attestation_evidence_jcs_b64.description: Wave 3b (design §4.2, P1-6): base64url of the JCS(evidence) the supervisor attested. Non-authoritative transport — the desktop re-verifies the attestation signature against its manifest `supervisor_attestation`-usage key before persisting/trusting.

attestation_signature_b64.description: Wave 3b (design §4.2, P1-6): base64url of the detached Ed25519 supervisor attestation over `attestation_evidence_jcs_b64`.

supervisor_attestation_key_id.description: Wave 3b (design §4.2, P1-6): the manifest `supervisor_attestation` key id the desktop resolves + re-verifies the attestation against.

run_id.description: Wave 3b (design §4.2, P1-6): forensic run binding — the supervisor's run id for this receipt.

execution_attempt_id.description: Wave 3b (design §4.2, P1-6): forensic run binding — the execution attempt id.

lease_id.description: Wave 3b (design §4.2, P1-6): forensic run binding — the execution lease id.

receipt.description: The governed-run receipt (from bro_supervisor.SupervisorResult). Carries the signed material for the DESKTOP to verify. There is no `verified` field — trust is a desktop signature check, never a bridge boolean.

allOf.comment: ok:true REQUIRES a non-null string result AND a receipt object (which carries the signed material the desktop verifies).
ok:false NEVER carries a result: result must be null.
